The rolling 24hr period (as per permit)  ending at plant off was 73.6 - duly reported to Agency.. Acknowledged in Q2 CAR form as no breach. (CAR Form Q2 2024 RP3004MA-0519663). Said 73.6mg/Nm3  used within calculation of above averages.

Note - the data suggests TOC on 18th November had one half hour at 20.1 however this was not an emission. During the routine, planned, 6 monthly service of the CEMS a filter is replaced which is located at the back of the sample probes for the duty & standby gas analysers. An issue with the filter indicated an increased TOC level, the 'elevated' time period on the duty was 12:57 - 13:16. The CO report and other data showed that there were no combustion issues at the time and that the indicated elevated TOC was due to the filter not due to plant process & waste combustion. Filters have been returned to manufacturer for investigation.  Issue duly reported in writing to site EA officers 19th November 2024.

From: Richard Cross <Richard.Cross@wastecare.co.uk> 
Sent: Thursday, May 16, 2024 4:15 PM
To: Megan Roberts <Megan.Roberts@forwardwaste.co.uk>
Subject: FORWARD PCI WASTE STREAMS

Hi Megan,
On another note Rebecca and the chemists team have noted the following about certain waste shipped into us here at EK.

To summarise, 
Following an emissions breach last week with Forward PCI waste we request that in future all yellow plastic tubs supplied into us MUST weigh a MAX of 10kgs.

This covers all of these containers
Which contain
Pharma (tablets, capsules, blister packs etc) 
Contaminated PPE
Glass Material.
If you could implement this with your client with immediate effect this would be very much appreciated.", "No Impact", null], ["Allow the plant to cool down to allow for access to the top of the SCC, check the integrity of the seal and replace if required.

[["Measures taken to prevent reoccurrence", "CCS score if applicable*", null], [null, "Impact", "Root cause"], ["Bag filter to be sent to third party for tests. This will identify why the bag filter failed.", 0.1, null], ["•	Daily part load waste checks
•	Weekly full load checks
•	Collaboration with Waste transfer stations
•	Actively working with waste collective authorities", 0.1, null], ["•	Daily part load waste checks 
•	Weekly full load checks 
•	Collaboration with Waste transfer stations 
•	Actively working with waste collective authorities - SUEZ hold meetings with the clients (Authorities) who bring waste into Tees Valley EfW’s and one of the agenda points is non-conforming waste, during the last meeting gas canister segregation was discussed. SUEZ advised the client the issues the plants have been having with regards to damage of the furnaces, and also the VOC breaches that have been happening.  The client is also going to write to their customers advising them of the issues that SUEZ are experiencing.  
•	Site have adjusted and trialling the damping time of the ID Fan to reduce trips from explosion.", 0.1, null], ["The following procedure will be updated in relation to the tripping of a lime screw/ system to ensure effective and consistent response in the future:
• S1-2OPSWP 4.3.3.5 Rising SO2 Levels
• S1-2OPSWP 4.3.3.6 Lime and Carbon storage and handling
• A TBT has also been drafted and distributed to shift operations personnel explaining the root causes of the issue from an awareness perspective and to further ensure consistent operational reaction to future issues. This is an interim communication measure to relevant staff with the applicable procedures as identified above also currently under review/update to reflect any learnings.", 0.1, null], ["The current procedure for clearing blockages from the superheater hopper, S1-2OPSWP 4.3.4.6 Rodding of super heater hopper, will be updated to be a more detailed document for all blockages on site including combustion air systems, and furnace hoppers, etc. This will give clear instruction and awareness of the potential for increase in air flow when a blockage is cleared and further clear instruction for the ID Fan to be in manual mode to prevent it from tripping", 0.1, null], ["•	A PM to be set up on Mainsaver to test run the redundant unit fortnightly. This will also be implemented for the Carbon System.", null, null], ["TV013/24 - The first CEM’s issue on 24th December 2024, was the result of the analyser reading above 100% moisture and 0% O2.
•	As the analyser corrects other measured variables for moisture, this caused the other readings to be inaccurate during this high moisture and a 0% O2 period.
•	Furthermore, the analysers ability to differentiate between H2O and O2 is less accurate when one of these values is severely outside of its normal range; in this case H20 was much too high, this had a major effect on the analysers ability to determine the O2 levels.
•	Finally, although the raw dust had a small spike (~4.9ppb) the corrected dust value increased disproportionately because of inaccurate H2O readings.
We now believe this is non reportable", null, null], ["•	Continue with waste spot checks – part loads and weekly full loads
•	Continue collaborating with Waste Transfer Stations, educating them on acceptable waste streams.", null, null], ["TV015/24 - The second CEM’s issue on 31st December 2024, was the result of abnormal moisture and O2 readings.
•	The analyser corrected the readings for the measured components using H2O, which was above normal range.
•	In addition, the reporting software corrected the measured components using O2, which was above normal range.
•	These inaccuracies combined resulting in the reported data for the components being incorrect. 
We now believe this is non reportable.", null, null]]
